_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true escalates to human review
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed'; use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: This repo (amos-labs/amos-protocol) had no CI at all. Add a green, meaningful CI pipeline plus the Plumbline proof-carrying gate. ci.yml adds a 'Lint & Unit Tests' job (cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test --lib and --doc) scoped to the cargo workspace (amos-core, amos-relay, amos-agent, amos-oracle), and a separate 'Anchor Build' job for the amos-solana on-chain programs (a separate cargo workspace requiring the Solana + Anchor toolchains, kept out of the fast fmt/clippy/test path and run advisory/continue-on-error). The Anchor job installs the exact anchor_version pinned in amos-solana/Anchor.toml (0.31.1) so `anchor build` resolves and succeeds. plumbline.yml adds the 'Plumbline Gate' check (full semantic review since ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set), with .plumbline/ scaffolding (policy.json, MISSION.md grounded in the protocol layer: bounty relay, Oracle semantic review, agent, on-chain programs).

validation_plan.reason: the ci.yml formatting step must pass on the current tree, or CI ships red
clippy runs warnings-as-errors in CI; confirm the workspace is clean so the new job is green and meaningful, not red
confirm the whole cargo workspace compiles offline with no DATABASE_URL — amos-relay uses runtime sqlx, no compile-checked query! macros, so no DB or .sqlx cache is needed in CI
the CI unit-test step must pass
the change is mostly YAML + JSON config; confirm both workflows parse

execution_evidence.output_ref: exit 0, Finished with no warnings across amos-core/relay/agent/oracle
exit 0, Finished dev profile (all 4 workspace crates)
both workflows parse; policy.json is valid JSON

execution_evidence.command: gh run view <ci-run> --json jobs (both CI jobs on PR head)
execution_evidence.output_ref: Lint & Unit Tests: success; Anchor Build: success (after pinning Anchor to Anchor.toml's 0.31.1) on PR head

result_summary: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml (jobs 'Lint & Unit Tests' and 'Anchor Build (on-chain programs)') and .github/workflows/plumbline.yml (check 'Plumbline Gate'), plus .plumbline/policy.json and MISSION.md. The cargo workspace (amos-core, amos-relay, amos-agent, amos-oracle) was verified green locally: fmt --check clean, clippy -D warnings clean, cargo check --all-targets OK offline (no DB needed — runtime sqlx only), and cargo test --lib = 177 passed / 0 failed. The amos-solana Anchor programs are a separate cargo workspace built via `anchor build` under the Solana + Anchor toolchains, so they are handled by a dedicated advisory job (continue-on-error) rather than dragged into the plain fmt/clippy/test path — keeping the required 'Lint & Unit Tests' check fast, green and meaningful. That Anchor job initially failed because it installed Anchor 0.30.1 while amos-solana/Anchor.toml pins anchor_version 0.31.1; pinning the install to the Anchor.toml version makes `anchor build` resolve and pass on CI. Touches .github/workflows/** and .plumbline/** (protected paths) so self_modifying:true; the gate escalates to human review by design.
